liteListings.name: PolarFire SoC DK — Complex Neural Network Accelerator
liteListings.description: Runs a larger model with batched execution on the PolarFire SoC to maximize hardware neural-network acceleration gains while reporting results to /IOTCONNECT.

liteListings.name: PolarFire SoC DK — Simple Neural Network Accelerator
liteListings.description: Demonstrates a compact fixed-point neural-network accelerator on the PolarFire SoC using the same /IOTCONNECT command interface as the template-correlation classifier.

liteListings.name: PolarFire SoC DK — Template Correlation Classifier
liteListings.description: Validates end-to-end cloud command/control and telemetry on the PolarFire SoC with a deterministic template-correlation classifier.

liteListings.name: PolarFire Video Kit — Complex Neural Network Accelerator
liteListings.description: Runs a larger model with batched execution on the PolarFire SoC Video Kit to maximize hardware neural-network acceleration gains.

liteListings.name: SAMA7D65 Curiosity — Keyword Spotting

liteListings.name: SAMA7D65 Curiosity — PAC1934 Power Monitoring
liteListings.description: Reads voltage, current, and power from the on-board PAC1934 power-monitor IC and publishes the measurements to /IOTCONNECT.

liteListings.name: i.MX93 — Driver Monitoring with Face Recognition

problems.item: PolarFire SoC Video Kit board ref vs lite listings
problems.issue: Internal board-reference inconsistency: the new board catalog entry carries manufacturer PN MPFS250-VIDEO-KIT, but the three Video Kit lite listings and the quickstart resource reference the board as MPFS-VIDEO-KIT. Both are valid identifiers, but catalog vs listings/quickstart use different ref strings for the same board, which may break a board-to-listing join. Confirm the join key is intentional.

problems.item: PolarFire npu topic tags (MPFS-DISCO-KIT and MPFS-VIDEO-KIT NN-accelerator listings)
problems.issue: Soft accuracy concern, not a vocab violation: npu is in the allowed vocabulary, but PolarFire SoC has no hardware NPU. NEW BOARDS tags it FPGA/RISC-V and the repo describes these demos as a neural network in FPGA fabric. Tagging FPGA-fabric NN-accelerator demos as npu is arguably misleading; edge-ai/ai alone may be more accurate.

problems.item: i.MX93 Driver Monitoring with Face Recognition link slug
problems.issue: Cosmetic only (path verified to exist): the folder slug dms_plus_face_recog-demo mixes underscores and a hyphen, unlike the consistently hyphenated slugs elsewhere. Link is valid, so no action strictly required.

verify.verdict: Mostly clean. All six NEW BOARDS part numbers verified correct against manufacturer/distributor sources (ABX00162 = Arduino UNO Q, MPFS250-VIDEO-KIT = PolarFire SoC Video Kit, 945-13730-0000-000 = Jetson AGX Orin Dev Kit, RTK9947E57S01000BE = RZ/G3E EVK, RTK0EF0168C04000BJ = RZ/V2H EVK, STM32MP215F-DK). No topics fall outside the allowed vocabulary. No duplicate listing names. All links follow the real github.com/avnet-iotconnect/.../tree/main/... pattern, and the non-obvious paths I spot-checked (i.MX93 face-recog, PolarFire VK track1/2/3, i.MX93 greengrass) actually exist. The items listed are minor/soft concerns rather than hard errors; no blocking problems found.
